finding: produced_visible_content reflected only the final loop iteration; a multi-iteration turn with visible content in an earlier iteration but an empty final iteration was flagged empty.
resolution: fixed — hoisted turn-level turn_produced_visible_content OR-accumulated across iterations

finding: extension-content loop hardcoded is_visible_answer(type, ""), relying on a comment-only assumption that extensions never emit a text block.
resolution: fixed — extract the actual text field from content_json before classifying

finding: is_visible_answer catch-all _ => true could false-negative a hypothetical non-rendered persisted content type.
resolution: wontfix — deliberate SAFE default (err toward NOT showing a spurious notice), identical to the frontend default: true; no invisible persisted content type exists in the vocabulary

finding: same multi-iteration per-accumulator scope as the correctness finding.
notice fired for ANY finalized answerless assistant turn, including stream-errored and user-cancelled partials, misattributing an interrupted turn as an empty response.
resolution: fixed — suppress via lastTurnInterrupted store flag threaded as the interrupted prop

finding: the !isStreaming gate (no-flash-mid-stream) was unverified by any test.
resolution: fixed — extracted shouldShowEmptyCompletionNotice pure fn + unit tests covering streaming/interrupted/user/answer cases

finding: multi-iteration path untested and exposed a backend/frontend divergence.
resolution: fixed — the divergence is eliminated by the turn-level hoist (backend now matches the frontend whole-message view); a dedicated multi-iteration integration test is deferred (needs an MCP tool round-trip); the OR logic + is_visible_answer are unit-tested and J re-verified the multi-iteration path reports NON-empty

finding: the fully-empty (no reasoning) case had no backend integration coverage.
the spec docstring overstated that the client consumes finish_reason:empty; the notice is render-time content-derived.
the notice gate ran un-memoized on every find-highlight re-render, inconsistent with the adjacent offerCollapse useMemo.
resolution: fixed — wrapped in useMemo with deps [isUser,isStreaming,interrupted,message]

finding: errored turn mislabeled as empty (duplicate of the correctness finding).
Alert tone=warning maps to role=alert with AA tokens; correct.
hardcoded English copy matches the repo (no i18n framework in the chat module).

lastTurnInterrupted is a single global field not reset on conversation switch / cache-restore and written even for background-conversation completions, so it leaks across conversations (stale suppress/show of the notice).
resolution: fixed — reset on switch (line ~718) + cache-hit restore (loadConversationState) + gate the complete-handler write on isOnOriginalConversation

finding: interrupted is transient client state gated to the last message and lost on reload, so an interrupted answerless turn still shows the notice after a reload or once pushed off last position.
resolution: accepted-limitation — inherent to the approved render-time content-based design (no persisted per-message marker); severity low; the notice is a reasonable generic 'no answer produced' indicator in that state. Documented in DECISIONS/FIX_ROUND. A full fix would require persisting the empty-vs-interrupted distinction, which the approved design excluded.

finding: useMemo gate deps correct and strictly beneficial; MessageList store read adds no meaningful re-renders; backend cost negligible.
the warn log carries only UUIDs + provider finish_reason, no content/PII.
finalize error path + accumulator lock unaffected by the new field; no new error branch swallowed.
single detached task drives the loop; produced_visible_content is read/written under the accumulator lock; turn_produced_visible_content is task-local — no data race.
finish_reason is a free-form String on SSEChatStreamCompleteData; 'empty' breaks no consumer (frontend complete handler does not branch on it; no schema/enum change).
the warn + descriptive finish_reason mirror the existing max_iterations/cancelled/extension_complete conventions; the Alert usage mirrors ConversationPage.
